title: rinorea angustifolia subsp. albersii
intro: Rinorea angustifolia subsp. albersii, also known as Albers' Rinorea, is a plant species in the family Violaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rinorea angustifolia subsp. albersii include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
